daily_fact: Rocketship added a 2026 New York City date—the indie band’s first NYC show in 12 years—giving local fans a long-awaited East Coast reunion.
topline_md: NYC’s latest cross-domain updates center on Mayor Mamdani’s agenda: housing and rent fights, a delayed racial equity plan, administrative moves and infrastructure milestones, alongside major social-service and justice developments including rising student homelessness, Bellevue replacing a Rikers medical unit, and a new ACS commissioner.

items.title: Bellevue jail unit opening as Rikers medical facility shuts
items.why_it_matters: The move affects jail healthcare capacity and the city’s long-delayed plan to close Rikers.

items.title: City eyes new controls on rent-stabilized apartment sales
items.why_it_matters: Potential new rules could reshape housing ownership, tenant protections, and investment in rent-stabilized buildings.

items.title: Mamdani releases delayed NYC racial equity plan
items.why_it_matters: The plan could influence agency spending, hiring, and service delivery across city government.

items.title: Mamdani names former Biden official to lead ACS
items.why_it_matters: Leadership at child welfare agency ACS directly affects vulnerable families, foster care, and abuse investigations.

items.title: Mamdani and Trump discuss Sunnyside Yard development
items.why_it_matters: Any revival of the project could affect housing supply, transit planning, and nearby Queens neighborhoods.

items.title: East New York affordable housing lottery opens
items.why_it_matters: New apartments can expand lower-cost housing options for Brooklyn residents facing high rents.

items.title: Report highlights school barriers for homeless NYC children
items.why_it_matters: Rising child homelessness disrupts attendance and signals worsening strain on families and city support systems.

items.title: NYC expands Rikers workforce training programs
items.why_it_matters: More job training for people in custody can improve reentry prospects and reduce future unemployment.

items.title: Citi Bike offers free unlimited rides for Earth Day
items.why_it_matters: The promotion gives New Yorkers a short-term free transit option and encourages low-cost travel.

items.title: Lafayette to get new two-way bike lane through Village and Soho
items.why_it_matters: The redesign will change street safety and travel patterns for cyclists, drivers, and pedestrians in Lower Manhattan.

items.title: Brooklyn vigil condemns shooting death of 7-month-old child
items.why_it_matters: The tragedy has intensified neighborhood grief and public concern about gun violence and child safety.

items.title: 101 outdoor dining cafes set to open on Upper West Side
items.why_it_matters: The openings will affect sidewalk use, neighborhood dining options, and street activity across the Upper West Side.

items.title: MoMA PS1 marks 50 years with free block party
items.why_it_matters: The free celebration offers Queens residents a major neighborhood arts event with broad public access.
